executive_summary: Build workflow dispatch failed with HTTP 404 error. The GitHub Actions workflow file (.github/workflows/build.yml) was not found or is not accessible in the repository. This is a critical configuration error preventing any build execution. Root cause: Workflow file missing, incorrect path, or insufficient permissions on the GitHub token to access Actions workflows.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found
errors.description: The workflow dispatch API endpoint returned HTTP 404 Not Found when attempting to trigger the build.yml workflow. This indicates the workflow file does not exist at the expected path (.github/workflows/build.yml) or the GitHub token lacks necessary permissions to access it.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={"message":"Not Found","documentation_url":"https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#create-a-workflow-dispatch-event","status":"404"}
errors.root_cause: Workflow file missing from repository or GitHub token lacks 'workflow' scope permissions. The API endpoint https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches returned 404, indicating either: (1) The workflow file does not exist at .github/workflows/build.yml, (2) The GitHub personal access token does not have 'workflow' or 'actions:write' permissions, or (3) GitHub Actions is disabled for this repository.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No builds can be triggered or executed until the workflow file is created or permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD automation, preventing code compilation, testing, and deployment.

steps: 1. Verify .github/workflows/ directory exists in repository root
2. Create build.yml workflow file with Maven build configuration
3. Ensure workflow includes 'workflow_dispatch' trigger for manual execution
4. Verify GitHub token has 'workflow' and 'actions:write' scopes
5. Confirm GitHub Actions is enabled in repository settings
recommended_fix.workflow_template:
name: Build

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      source_folder:
        description: 'Source folder to build'
        required: true
        default: 'lld'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Set up JDK 11
        uses: actions/setup-java@v3
        with:
          java-version: '11'
          distribution: 'temurin'
      - name: Build with Maven
        run: mvn clean install -f ${{ github.event.inputs.source_folder }}/pom.xml
recommended_fix.compliance_note: Workflow file creation requires repository write access. Ensure change is tracked in version control and reviewed per organizational policies.

errors.title: GitHub Token Permission Insufficient
errors.description: The GitHub personal access token may lack the required 'workflow' scope to dispatch workflow events. The 404 error can occur when the token has 'repo' access but not 'workflow' or 'actions:write' permissions.
errors.error_message: Token missing workflow/actions permissions. API documentation: https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#create-a-workflow-dispatch-event
errors.root_cause: GitHub PAT was created without 'workflow' scope enabled. The token may only have 'repo' scope, which is insufficient for triggering workflow dispatches via the Actions API.
errors.impact: Unable to programmatically trigger GitHub Actions workflows. Manual workflow runs may still work through the GitHub UI, but automated CI/CD pipelines cannot dispatch builds.

steps: 1. Navigate to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
3. Edit token and enable 'workflow' scope checkbox
recommended_fix.compliance_note: Token permission changes must be documented and approved by security team. Follow least-privilege principle.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Potentially Disabled
errors.description: GitHub Actions may be disabled at the repository or organization level, preventing workflow execution even if the workflow file exists.
errors.error_message: Actions disabled. Workflow not accessible on this repo/branch.
errors.root_cause: Repository owner or organization administrator may have disabled GitHub Actions in repository settings, or Actions may be restricted to specific workflows only.
errors.impact: All GitHub Actions workflows are non-functional. CI/CD pipeline completely blocked until Actions are re-enabled.

steps: 1. Navigate to repository Settings > Actions > General
2. Verify 'Actions permissions' is set to 'Allow all actions and reusable workflows'
3. Check 'Workflow permissions' has 'Read and write permissions' enabled
4. Confirm no organization-level restrictions are blocking Actions
recommended_fix.compliance_note: Enabling Actions requires repository admin permissions. Document change in audit log.

summary.failure_reason: Workflow file not found or inaccessible (HTTP 404)

compliance_audit.analysis_method: Automated error classification from build failure logs
compliance_audit.manual_review_reason: Critical configuration error requires admin-level permissions to resolve. Workflow file creation and token permission updates need human approval.
compliance_notes: Workflow file creation requires code review and approval per SDLC policy
GitHub token permission changes must be logged in security audit trail
Actions enablement requires repository admin approval
All configuration changes must be documented in change management system

next_steps: 1. Create .github/workflows/build.yml file with Maven build configuration
2. Update GitHub PAT to include 'workflow' scope permissions
3. Verify GitHub Actions is enabled in repository settings
5. Monitor build execution and verify successful completion
